name: Fly.io API
what_it_does: Fly.io's Machines REST API for launching, managing, and scaling containerized applications globally across Fly.io's edge network.
use_cases: Programmatically launching and managing container instances (Machines)
Auto-scaling applications based on demand from agent orchestrators
Deploying and updating containerized workloads via automation
Managing app configuration, secrets, and volumes
Running ephemeral compute jobs in containers globally
not_for: Teams needing managed Kubernetes (use GKE/EKS instead)
Simple static site hosting (overkill compared to Netlify/Vercel)
best_when: An agent needs to launch and manage containerized workloads on Fly.io, especially ephemeral compute or globally distributed apps.
avoid_when: You need managed Kubernetes, are not using containers, or need a simpler static hosting solution.

auth.notes: Auth tokens generated via flyctl CLI or web dashboard. Single token grants full account/app access. No fine-grained scopes. Simple bearer token auth for agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free allowance: 3 shared-cpu-1x VMs, 3GB storage, 160GB transfer
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay per machine-second, starting ~$0.0000017/second for shared CPU
pricing.notes: Very granular per-second billing. Free allowance covers small projects. Credit card required even for free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented, generous in practice

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Machines API returns JSON errors with status and error fields. Documentation is developer-friendly but API is lower-level than Vercel/Netlify. GraphQL API has different error format.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Machine creation is not idempotent. Agents must track machine IDs to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Credit card required even for free tier — blocks quick experimentation
No official SDK — agents must use raw HTTP or flyctl CLI
Machines API is lower-level than most PaaS APIs — requires more agent intelligence
Two APIs coexist: Machines REST API and older GraphQL API with inconsistent coverage
No webhooks — agents must poll for machine state changes
Secrets management requires flyctl CLI for encryption — API access to secrets is limited

security_score_components.security_notes: App deployment platform. Fly.io auth token. Deploy tokens with org/app scope. App secrets stored in Fly vault. SSH access to machines requires separate key.
